title: STANDARD FOR SPECIAL DIETARY FOODS WITH LOW-SODIUM CONTENT (INCLUDING SALT SUBSTITUTES)

sections.section: STANDARD FOR SPECIAL DIETARY FOODS WITH LOW-SODIUM CONTENT (INCLUDING SALT SUBSTITUTES)
sections.summary: Document title: Standard for special dietary foods with low‑sodium content, including salt substitutes.
Adoption history: adopted in 1981 and amended in 1983 and 2019.

Applies to foods represented as intended for special dietary uses because of low sodium, including low‑sodium salt substitutes, and contains only provisions specific to sodium content and salt substitutes, not other compositional aspects or use of food additives (except for salt substitutes).

Defines 'special dietary foods with low sodium content' as foods whose special dietary value results from reduction, restriction or removal of sodium, and states that the terms 'low sodium' and 'very low sodium' refer to foods meeting the maximum sodium content provisions in subsections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2.

Sets compositional criteria: 'low sodium' and 'very low sodium' foods must be processed without added sodium salts, have ≤ one half the sodium of a comparable normal product and contain ≤120 mg/100 g and ≤40 mg/100 g respectively, and permits addition of salt substitutes limited by good manufacturing practice; it also defines permitted salt-substitute ingredients and restrictions (including specified cation/element percentage limits, magnesium and choline limits, colloidal silica ≤1% m/m, allowable nutritive diluents), requires iodine additions to follow national legislation, and limits sodium in salt substitutes to ≤120 mg/100 g.

Specifies labelling requirements for low-sodium special dietary foods and salt substitutes: labels must state 'low sodium' or 'very low sodium' per the standard, declare sodium to the nearest 5 mg/100 g and per serving, give average carbohydrate/protein/fat and energy values, declare added salt substitutes and potassium content if present, and include date-of-minimum-durability and storage instructions; for salt substitutes the product name must be 'low sodium salt substitute' or 'low sodium dietetic salt' and the label must list ingredients and per-100 g cation amounts, in addition to applicable general labelling provisions.

sections.section: 4.1 Special Dietary Foods with Low Sodium Content (Excluding Salt Substitutes As Such)
sections.summary: Requires specific labelling for special dietary foods with low sodium: display 'low sodium' or 'very low sodium' per definitions, declare sodium (nearest 5 mg/100 g and per serving), declare carbohydrate/protein/fat and energy per 100 g, declare any salt substitutes and total potassium (mg cation/100 g) if potassium salts are used, and provide 'best before' date and any storage conditions.

For salt substitutes, mandates the product name 'low sodium salt substitute' or 'low sodium dietetic salt' and requires a complete ingredient list plus declaration of the amounts of cations (sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium, ammonium and choline) per 100 g of the mixture.

Specifies that compliance with this Standard shall be checked using the methods of analysis and sampling referenced in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) relevant to the Standard's provisions.
